name: AWS WAF Query String Length Restriction (Abuse/DoS Reduction)
author: waf-defense-rulepacks
threats_mitigated: parser abuse via oversized query strings
resource pressure from unusually large URI query components
objective: Detect and mitigate requests with abnormally large query strings to reduce abuse and DoS-style probing against public endpoints.
risk_mitigated: Large query strings can stress application parsing, trigger edge-case behavior, and increase upstream resource consumption. Restricting query size narrows abuse surface with low implementation complexity.

scope_down_statement.note: Optionally exclude known-safe endpoints or constrain to internet-facing paths only.

prerequisites: AWS WAFv2 Web ACL attached to CloudFront, ALB, API Gateway, or AppSync
WAF logging enabled (recommended) to validate baseline query lengths before BLOCK mode
deployment_notes: Start in COUNT mode for at least 7 days to establish a baseline and identify legitimate large-query workflows.
Review top offending URIs, user agents, and source IP ranges before promoting to BLOCK.
If false positives are found, add targeted exclusions (for specific endpoints) rather than raising threshold globally.
Promote to BLOCK only after measured false-positive rate is acceptable for business traffic.
tuning_notes: Use 2048 bytes as a conservative initial threshold for mixed workloads; tighten toward 1024 where feasible.
Public APIs with encoded filters/search parameters may need endpoint-specific exceptions.
Keep transformations as NONE for direct size measurement consistency.
Pair with request body size controls and rate limits for stronger abuse resistance.

key_metrics: Source IP and ASN concentration of triggered traffic
alerts: Sudden spike in counted oversized-query requests (>3x baseline)
Unexpected increase in blocked requests on critical application paths

positive_tests: Send request with query string > threshold and verify COUNT/BLOCK behavior based on configured action.
Confirm normal requests below threshold are unaffected.
negative_tests: Validate critical application endpoints with typical and peak legitimate query lengths.
Confirm allowlisted/excluded endpoints (if configured) do not trigger this rule.

rollback.strategy: Switch rule action from BLOCK to COUNT, then disable if needed.
notes: Retain logging during rollback to continue root-cause and threshold analysis.
Prefer temporary endpoint exclusions over full rule removal when possible.
